title: File Read and Write
description: Write a program that creates a file named "output.txt", writes the text "Hello, file!" to it, then opens the file, reads its contents, and prints them to the screen.
starter_code: # Write your file handling code here

code_requirements.description: Opens a file in write mode
code_requirements.fail_message: Make sure you open a file with write mode ('w') to create and write to it.

code_requirements.description: Uses the with statement to open a file
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the 'with' statement to open files — it automatically handles closing.
Use .read() or .readlines() to read the contents of the file after writing.

hints: Use 'with' to open the file in write ('w') mode, then call .write('Hello, file!')
In a separate 'with' block, open the same file in read ('r') mode and call .read()
Pass the result of .read() directly to print() to display it
